    41→// RESTStorageProvider is a factory type for REST storage.
    42→type RESTStorageProvider interface {
    43→	GroupName() string
    44→	NewRESTStorage(apiResourceConfigSource serverstorage.APIResourceConfigSource, restOptionsGetter generic.RESTOptionsGetter) (genericapiserver.APIGroupInfo, error)
    45→}
    46→
    47→// NewCoreGenericConfig returns a new core rest generic config.
    48→func (c *CompletedConfig) NewCoreGenericConfig() *corerest.GenericConfig {
    49→	return &corerest.GenericConfig{
    50→		StorageFactory:              c.Extra.StorageFactory,
    51→		EventTTL:                    c.Extra.EventTTL,
    52→		LoopbackClientConfig:        c.Generic.LoopbackClientConfig,
    53→		ServiceAccountIssuer:        c.Extra.ServiceAccountIssuer,
    54→		ExtendExpiration:            c.Extra.ExtendExpiration,
    55→		IsTokenSignerExternal:       c.Extra.IsTokenSignerExternal,
    56→		ServiceAccountMaxExpiration: c.Extra.ServiceAccountMaxExpiration,
    57→		APIAudiences:                c.Generic.Authentication.APIAudiences,
    58→		Informers:                   c.Extra.VersionedInformers,
    59→	}
    60→}
    61→
    62→// GenericStorageProviders returns a set of APIs for a generic control plane.
    63→// They ought to be a subset of those served by kube-apiserver.
    64→func (c *CompletedConfig) GenericStorageProviders(discovery discovery.DiscoveryInterface) ([]RESTStorageProvider, error) {
    65→	// The order here is preserved in discovery.
    66→	// If resources with identical names exist in more than one of these groups (e.g. "deployments.apps"" and "deployments.extensions"),
    67→	// the order of this list determines which group an unqualified resource name (e.g. "deployments") should prefer.
    68→	// This priority order is used for local discovery, but it ends up aggregated in `k8s.io/kubernetes/cmd/kube-apiserver/app/aggregator.go
    69→	// with specific priorities.
    70→	// TODO: describe the priority all the way down in the RESTStorageProviders and plumb it back through the various discovery
    71→	// handlers that we have.
    72→	return []RESTStorageProvider{
    73→		c.NewCoreGenericConfig(),
    74→		apiserverinternalrest.StorageProvider{},
    75→		authenticationrest.RESTStorageProvider{Authenticator: c.Generic.Authentication.Authenticator, APIAudiences: c.Generic.Authentication.APIAudiences},
    76→		authorizationrest.RESTStorageProvider{Authorizer: c.Generic.Authorization.Authorizer, RuleResolver: c.Generic.RuleResolver},
    77→		certificatesrest.RESTStorageProvider{},
    78→		coordinationrest.RESTStorageProvider{},
    79→		rbacrest.RESTStorageProvider{Authorizer: c.Generic.Authorization.Authorizer},
    80→		svmrest.RESTStorageProvider{},
    81→		flowcontrolrest.RESTStorageProvider{InformerFactory: c.Generic.SharedInformerFactory},
    82→		admissionregistrationrest.RESTStorageProvider{Authorizer: c.Generic.Authorization.Authorizer, DiscoveryClient: discovery},
    83→		eventsrest.RESTStorageProvider{TTL: c.EventTTL},
    84→	}, nil
    85→}
    86→
    87→// InstallAPIs will install the APIs for the restStorageProviders if they are enabled.
    88→func (s *Server) InstallAPIs(restStorageProviders ...RESTStorageProvider) error {
    89→	nonLegacy := []*genericapiserver.APIGroupInfo{}
    90→
    91→	// used later in the loop to filter the served resource by those that have expired.
    92→	resourceExpirationEvaluator, err := genericapiserver.NewResourceExpirationEvaluator(s.GenericAPIServer.EffectiveVersion.EmulationVersion())
    93→	if err != nil {
    94→		return err
    95→	}
    96→
    97→	for _, restStorageBuilder := range restStorageProviders {
    98→		groupName := restStorageBuilder.GroupName()
    99→		apiGroupInfo, err := restStorageBuilder.NewRESTStorage(s.APIResourceConfigSource, s.RESTOptionsGetter)
   100→		if err != nil {
   101→			return fmt.Errorf("problem initializing API group %q: %w", groupName, err)
   102→		}
   103→		if len(apiGroupInfo.VersionedResourcesStorageMap) == 0 {
   104→			// If we have no storage for any resource configured, this API group is effectively disabled.
   105→			// This can happen when an entire API group, version, or development-stage (alpha, beta, GA) is disabled.
   106→			klog.Infof("API group %q is not enabled, skipping.", groupName)
   107→			continue
   108→		}
   109→
   110→		// Remove resources that serving kinds that are removed.
   111→		// We do this here so that we don't accidentally serve versions without resources or openapi information that for kinds we don't serve.
   112→		// This is a spot above the construction of individual storage handlers so that no sig accidentally forgets to check.
   113→		resourceExpirationEvaluator.RemoveDeletedKinds(groupName, apiGroupInfo.Scheme, apiGroupInfo.VersionedResourcesStorageMap)
   114→		if len(apiGroupInfo.VersionedResourcesStorageMap) == 0 {
   115→			klog.V(1).Infof("Removing API group %v because it is time to stop serving it because it has no versions per APILifecycle.", groupName)
   116→			continue
   117→		}
   118→
   119→		klog.V(1).Infof("Enabling API group %q.", groupName)
   120→
   121→		if postHookProvider, ok := restStorageBuilder.(genericapiserver.PostStartHookProvider); ok {
   122→			name, hook, err := postHookProvider.PostStartHook()
   123→			if err != nil {
   124→				return fmt.Errorf("error building PostStartHook: %w", err)
   125→			}
   126→			s.GenericAPIServer.AddPostStartHookOrDie(name, hook)
   127→		}
   128→
   129→		if len(groupName) == 0 {
   130→			// the legacy group for core APIs is special that it is installed into /api via this special install method.
   131→			if err := s.GenericAPIServer.InstallLegacyAPIGroup(genericapiserver.DefaultLegacyAPIPrefix, &apiGroupInfo); err != nil {
   132→				return fmt.Errorf("error in registering legacy API: %w", err)
   133→			}
   134→		} else {
   135→			// everything else goes to /apis
   136→			nonLegacy = append(nonLegacy, &apiGroupInfo)
   137→		}
   138→	}
   139→
   140→	if err := s.GenericAPIServer.InstallAPIGroups(nonLegacy...); err != nil {
   141→		return fmt.Errorf("error in registering group versions: %w", err)
   142→	}
   143→	return nil
   144→}
